BMH HMS Secure Access
Login once, continue work across reception, doctors, billing and OT.
Use your BMH username and password to enter the hospital management system. If this is your personal workstation, you can save the password for faster sign-in next time.
Role-aware access
Admins, reception, doctors and lab users land in their own workspace automatically.
Centre-aware setup
Chandigarh and Ropar accounts stay locked to the correct centre after sign-in.
Saved passwords
Turn on save-password only on trusted devices so returning staff can log in quickly.

📋 Advice to Patient
Eye Care:
Avoid rubbing eyes Use dark glasses outdoors Complete eye drops course Avoid swimming Rest eyes — limit screen time No driving after dilation
General Health:
Control blood sugar Control blood pressure Pre-op fasting (after midnight) Head elevated post-op Protective shield at night

Level II USG · 20 Wks · 10 Dec 2024
Single live intrauterine fetus, cephalic. All fetal organs normal. No anomaly detected. Placenta posterior, grade I. AFI 12 cm.

Select a patient to view notes
22 Mar 2025 — Visit 3
PHQ-9: 17
Patient reports slight improvement in sleep. Mood still low. Appetite improved. Escitalopram 10mg — good tolerance, no side effects. Continue same. CBT session 3 completed — thought records started. Review in 4 weeks.
22 Feb 2025 — Visit 2
PHQ-9: 20
Persistent low mood, anhedonia. Sleep slightly improved on Clonazepam. Started Escitalopram 10mg today. CBT session 1 done. Psycho-education to patient and family.

Chemical Peel (Glycolic 30%)
Session 1 of 4 · 15 Mar 2025 · Dr. Pooja Baweja

Pending eval
Lifestyle & Home Care Advice
☀️ Strict sun avoidance — wear hat + SPF 50+ daily
🚫 Stop all OTC fairness creams with steroids
💊 Oral tranexamic acid 250mg BD × 3 months
🥗 Antioxidant-rich diet — Vit C, E supplements
💧 Hydrate well — 2–3L water/day
🚭 No smoking — worsens pigmentation
🌙 Skincare routine: Cleanser → Tretinoin (night) → Moisturiser → SPF (morning)

📥 Import Patients from CSV
Supports the standard Docterz/BMH CSV format. Duplicate patients (matching mobile or UID) will be skipped.
Expected columns: Name, Age, Sex, DOB, Mobile, Email, Address, Patient UID, Significant History, Past History, Allergy History

✅ WHO Safe Surgery Checklist
Select case to auto-populate patient details
Sign In (Pre-op)
Time Out (Before incision)
Sign Out (Post-op)
Sign In — Before Anaesthesia
Patient confirmed identity, site, procedure and consent
Surgical site marked (where applicable)
Anaesthesia machine and medication check complete
Pulse oximeter on patient and functioning
Known allergy?
Difficult airway / aspiration risk?
Risk of >500ml blood loss?
Eye to be operated — confirmed and marked?
Time Out — Before Skin Incision (ALL team members confirm)
All team members have introduced themselves by name and role
Confirm patient name, procedure and site
Confirm anticipated critical events — Surgeon
Confirm anticipated critical events — Anaesthesia team
Confirm anticipated critical events — Nursing team
Antibiotic prophylaxis given within last 60 minutes?
Essential imaging displayed?
Implant / IOL model confirmed?
Microscope / equipment checked and functional?
Sign Out — Before Patient Leaves OT
Nurse verbally confirms: name of procedure recorded
Instrument, sponge and needle counts correct?
Specimen labelled (including patient name)?
Any equipment problems to address?
Surgeon, anaesthetist, nurse: key concerns for recovery?
IOL sticker / implant label attached to OT notes?
